title: Runtime Configuration, Secrets, Environment Precedence, and Sensitive Data · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Secret Leak Surfaces
summary: A service must receive database credentials and rotation-safe settings without baking them into images, logs, history, or source control. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker secret leak surfaces increment in configuration and secret-flow contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure.
Grant only named services access to declared secrets and configs and verify file path, permissions, rotation, and local Compose limitations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Secret Leak Surfaces · Theory Docker Secret Leak Surfaces
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Secret Leak Surfaces contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Secret Leak Surfaces · Theory Docker Secret Leak Surfaces.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure. For Predict Docker Secret Leak Surfaces · Theory Docker Secret Leak Surfaces, keep Docker Secret Leak Surfaces connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure. A defensible solution applies Docker Secret Leak Surfaces, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to configuration and secret-flow contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-secret-leak-surfaces — Audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-config-secrets-environment-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-config-secrets-environment-4
    artifact: configuration-boundary.yaml
    decision: worked docker config secrets environment 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-environment-map
      runtime-secret: mounted-file
      build-secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      logs: redact-values
      rotation: restart-with-versioned-reference
    verify: docker compose config --environment && docker image history --no-trunc candidate
    failure: Reject secrets in Dockerfile ENV, ARG, Compose interpolation output, image history, repository files, or diagnostic logs.
    repair: Separate public config from secrets, inject each at the narrowest lifetime, scan metadata, rotate the credential, and verify redaction.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting a secret in a later image layer removes it from earlier layer content. This fails the Docker Secret Leak Surfaces contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
